title: Sketch -> extrude Blind happy path (NewBody)
source: OneCAD-CPP/tests/prototypes/proto_regeneration.cpp:151-218 — Test 1 'Single extrude regeneration': 10x10 sketch, Blind extrude distance 20, NewBody.
OneCAD-CPP/src/core/sketch/Sketch.h SketchPlane::XY() — non-standard basis xAxis(0,1,0) yAxis(-1,0,0) normal(0,0,1); default Sketch() ctor uses XY.
expected-values/proto_regeneration.txt — 'Test 1: Single extrude regeneration... PASS', exit 0.

perStep.note: sketch materialized; one closed region detected (LoopDetector). No body.

faceCount.note: A prism from a 4-line rectangle is a box => 6 planar faces / 12 edges / 8 vertices. The C++ test does NOT assert topology counts here (only volume + validity via shapeValid). Recorded as derived geometry, verify against worker output; treat volume as the load-bearing assertion.

notes: Simplest golden. BodyId 'body_1' is illustrative (Rust mints the real opaque id). The extrude input is the sketch's first detected region; in the protocol this is a semanticRef of kind face/region to the sketch region. Volume 2000 and single created body are the hard expectations.
